Dread Forged Shield  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 17
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 CHA: +10 WIS: +5 MANA: +20
SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 1.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: CLR
Race: HUM ERU
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Dread Forged Shield
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 17
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 CHA: +10 WIS: +5 MANA: +20
SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 1.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: CLR
Race: HUM ERU
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:Shield
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Stylean SilverSteel
Lucy Entry By:adetia (township rebellion)
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 05:31:50
Page Updated:Sat Jul 17th, 2021

Expansion: Original
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 40
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Summoning of the Dread

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Paineel


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Race/Class?
QuoteReply
# Jan 20 2005 at 7:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591820193229
19 posts
Seems odd that this is HUM,ERU. It should either be usable by all Cazic-Thule priests, including Troll and Iksar Shamans. Or all evil priests 0or at least clerics) should have access. The Dark Elves get Initiate/Disciple/Regent quests so they aren't exactly getting the shaft here. I just started a Gnome Bertoxx Cleric and it sucks that i can't use this. Bertoxx only has an Initiate symbol. At least Cazic worshippers get a Disciple symol.
▲ Page top
Forget it
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2004 at 11:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591798972587
Scholar
215 posts
I was really thinking about questing for this shield till I checked the bazaar. Ended up buying a Crystal Webshield for 45p instead.

The amount of effort to get both the Powder and the Brain would be incredible. Many human clerics have to faction like crazy in the Warrens too. Even if you have a high-level character who can help MQ it, shoot, why not just buy him something like the linked shield?

My character journal
▲ Page top
RE: Forget it
QuoteReply
# Jul 25 2004 at 2:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592761838153
144 posts
Because this shield is way better than the linked shiled.It's stats would be nice for a lower level cleric and the quest is pretty easy
▲ Page top
RE: Forget it
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2004 at 3:34 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
The brain is easy. Just hook up with an SK or two who need their summon corpse spell from ishva mal, and you'll have it in 30 min or so.

Everything else but the powder is common stuff often found on merchants.

True its easy to buy a similar item, but its kind of nice to have an item nobody has seen before.
▲ Page top
ToVs
QuoteReply
# Jul 14 2002 at 7:33 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591778490362
52 posts
its a decent quest but as alwats for a secindary item im going to suggest you get a testement of vanaer its not THAT long a wait
▲ Page top
Dread Forged Shield
QuoteReply
# Jun 15 2002 at 3:30 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592249445235
3 posts
I just completed my quest for the Dread Forged Shield. Ishva was a fun had 3 groups goin to kick doggy **** in Paw. The Re-animation powder was the confusing part, I could not get the gnome to respond to me he kept telling me to not give away his hiding spot, so I said oh well and handed the gnome in the closet my Greater lightstone and right away the abomination was screaming. it poped way down the hall behind the gnome, our tracker tracked it thru the door and we proceded to kill it. nice lil fight. The ice giant toes were by far the most easy part. I just got a tp to surefall and ran to the Icegiant camp in everfrost and a nice soul gave me a set off one of his kills. and on to the eye. now that was a lil bit of a challange. I had no group for this but got to loot one cause of another nice soul. there is my story hope it helps if your having problems or are just confused.
I was 34th lvl when I finished this quest.

Anoura
35th Cleric of Cazic-Thule
Lightkeepers of Gaia
Torv
▲ Page top
How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 6:37 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761744940
3 posts
I'm a 52 lvl human cleric if Marr. To raise faction kill Kobolds in the Warrens. I killed 1747 Kobolds to get enouph faction. I tracked that with EQdiary, a log parser. In any case 1747 kills later was enouph to get a key from the Paineel guards, to get into Paineel, to do the quest. Remember there is anti-train code in the warrens. You can have no more the 3 mobs on you at any one time. What I would do was agro 3 who would then follow me, then run near a group of 3 or 4 others, cast an AoE spell which kill them all, and get 5 to 7 faction hits per cast, rinse and repeate. The best place to do this was back by the prince. Not many hunt near prince so you are not taking kills for the lower level people there.
▲ Page top
RE: How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Nov 03 2002 at 5:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783343251
4 posts
Im sure this has been changed because I have been able to drag as much as 48 kobolds with me, I only take the ones with less than ~200 hp so my AoE will finish all of them off in one cast...I usually keep it to 20 or so at a time, but I could still get my spells off with 48 without worrying about having to zone because of the occasional lucky punk that interrupts.
I was lvl 39 when I was doing my faction.
Took no more than 4 1/2 hours game time to raise from kos to amiable, and this is with pulling them all to the door for newbies to loot, and helping them and others that came in to kill the froggy.

Gutentaug
41st Cleric - Rallos Zek
▲ Page top
RE: How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Feb 17 2002 at 3:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758231582
7 posts
1700 kobolds OMG... Try killing a guy in a hut ... not sure of location but it's near the back wall. Anyways this guy is holding a nice CHA +10 staff and is accompanied by an erudite female. Who will just stand there and watch as you kill this guy. I killed him 17 times and had enough faction to get the key for paineel. If you want the CHA staff you have to disarm him. This guy cons green to my 47 DEF SK.
▲ Page top
RE: How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2001 at 11:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757469021
Scholar
24 posts
I too am a Human cleric of Marr. It only took me approx 1100 +Heretic faction hits in the Warrens to get from scowling to amiable (note, the guard will NOT give you a Paineel key if you are indifferent). I had also taken a -Heretic for killing Azzar once when he got in my way :) Maybe initial faction standings are different for Mithaniel Marr vs Erollisi Marr (I am with Erollisi)
▲ Page top
RE: How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2001 at 6:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361599044
3 posts
On a note, you do not have to kill that many Kobolds in order to complete the quest. I killed about 400 to 500 to get faction to 'Dubious'. Then have someone in town open the elevator for you, and you can save alot of time on these quests. Gate out when done. These quests work perfectly on dubious faction.

Phim Starburner
54 Cleric
<Darkholme>
Saryrn
▲ Page top
RE: How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2001 at 9:02 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
What about the 'Ice Giant Toes'? I am a Human following Mithaniel Marr also. I would hate to kill 1747 Kobolds only to turn in my goods and find out I had the wrong toes! : ( > Plz reply, I am killing Kobolds in Tox Forest on The Seventh Hammer as we speak! hehe

Roiel Deadcrusher 45 Cleric<Circle of the Ancients>
▲ Page top
RE: How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2001 at 6:01 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361599044
3 posts
Another good source of giant toes is from Gornit in Ocean of Tears. He's a hill giant on the seafury island. He drops all 3 types of Giant toes of the old world. Ice, Fire, Hill. I used toes from Gornit to complete my quest. Took about 30 minutes camping him to get. Also, he can be soloed easily by a 44+ cleric, unlike an Ice Giant.

Phim Starburner
54 Cleric
<Darkholme>
Saryrn
▲ Page top
RE: How to Get shield
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2001 at 5:51 PM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
I got the toes from a group hunting in Everfrost.

I have all components
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2001 at 11:40 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I grouped and whooped on Ishva Last night, but am worried about the differences I am hearing on the "Ice Giant Toes". I would go and ask the quest fellow to give me the exact quote, but MOST likely am KOS and plan to spend a few day in Tox whooping guards to get faction back up. Anyone who has recieved this shield, could you please specify which toes worked? The Brain is no drop, the Eye is no drop, the Powder of Reanimation is no drop, but yet Ice giant toes drop all the time from IG's in Everfrost, and are not no drop?

Thanks,

Roiel Deadcrusher<Circle of the Ancients> The Seventh Hammer 45th Lev : ) >
▲ Page top
u can do it
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2001 at 1:33 PM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
um u can find a eru or human cleric with the powder turn in all your parts and let him turn in the powder and he gets the shield

now i don't even want to hear about that, ye who can get a neck item with clickable clinging darkness (a snare/dot) that has 7 ac and +3 wis. i'd give up this shield remembering how many times i would have killed for a no mana snare (even if we got snare in the first place).

Well as a human cleric of Innoruuk, I get to laugh now:

Muahahha

Got the Ishva last night and started the path to the shield, only need one piece for the mask and already got my snare necklace from Neriak.

I would be grateful to read any tips on handling the re-animation powder encounter, my experience in Unrest thus far has been short and unpleasant.

Gray Hateborn
Master of the League
Regent of Innoruuk
